headline: USPS to retain 80% of its Amazon deliveries under new deal
summary: STORY: Amazon said it reached a new agreement with the U.S. Postal Service on package deliveries.Sources said the deal allows the cash-strapped mail system to retain about 80% of its existing deliveries from Amazon, its biggest customer.That's a dramatically better outcome for USPS than the two-thirds or larger cut to deliveries Amazon had threatened, according to a Reuters report last month.USPS had warned that it could run out of cash as soon as October, and the risk that Amazon would replace the carrier by expanding its own delivery network or using rivals was an existential threat.Sources said Amazon will continue to expand its delivery network, but not to the point of rivaling the U.S. Postal Service.  USPS didn’t immediately comment on the new agreement.

raw.headline: USPS to retain 80% of its Amazon deliveries under new deal
raw.summary: STORY: Amazon said it reached a new agreement with the U.S. Postal Service on package deliveries.Sources said the deal allows the cash-strapped mail system to retain about 80% of its existing deliveries from Amazon, its biggest customer.That's a dramatically better outcome for USPS than the two-thirds or larger cut to deliveries Amazon had threatened, according to a Reuters report last month.USPS had warned that it could run out of cash as soon as October, and the risk that Amazon would replace the carrier by expanding its own delivery network or using rivals was an existential threat.Sources said Amazon will continue to expand its delivery network, but not to the point of rivaling the U.S. Postal Service.  USPS didn’t immediately comment on the new agreement.

headline: Uber bets on Amazon's custom chips to boost AI efforts
summary: Uber is using Amazon's custom chips to ‌speed up computing and ‌train artificial intelligence models, the cloud ​giant said on Tuesday, as the ride-hailing firm seeks advanced hardware to handle growing digital workloads. The ‌deal expands ⁠the companies' existing cloud partnership by enabling Uber ⁠to use Amazon Web Services' Graviton chips to support smoother ​rides and ​deliveries and ​Trainium processors to ‌train AI models that power its apps. Uber is working to optimize its digital interface, accelerate ride-matching and personalize user experiences ‌to attract users ​and gain a ​competitive edge.

headline: Goldman Sachs Says It’s Time to Buy Tech Stocks. Here’s Why.
summary: Big Tech stocks have taken a battering lately—but investors should take the opportunity to buy the dip as the Iran war drags on, according to Goldman Sachs.  Tech in 2026 has posted one of its worst periods of relative underperformance of the past 50 years amid a pivot to value stocks, analyst Peter Oppenheimer said in a research note on Tuesday.  Facebook owner  Meta Platforms  IT company  Microsoft  and chip maker  Nvidia  are all fetching less than 20 times expected earnings for the next 24 months, the analyst noted.

headline: Is Amazon (AMZN) Quietly Redefining Its Moat Through Chips, Credit Cards, and Satellites?
summary: COSRX recently launched its upgraded One Step Pad collection and PDRN eye patches on Amazon in the U.S., while Amazon has also unveiled new co-branded small-business credit cards and reached a revised parcel-delivery agreement that keeps most of its volume with the U.S. Postal Service. At the same time, reports that Amazon is exploring an acquisition of satellite operator Globalstar highlight how the company is using partnerships, financial products, and potential LEO assets to deepen its...

headline: Amazon Data Center Water Concerns Put ESG In Focus For Investors
summary: Amazon.com (NasdaqGS:AMZN) is facing increased shareholder pressure over the water usage and environmental impact of its expanding data center operations. A coalition of investors is pushing for greater transparency and stronger water conservation measures as Amazon scales infrastructure to support computing and AI workloads. Community resistance has already contributed to the cancellation of several multibillion dollar data center projects involving Amazon and its peers. The shareholder...

headline: Can $3.5B Legal Bottleneck Threaten EchoStar’s $20B SpaceX Lifeline
summary: EchoStar (SATS) has pulled off the ultimate pivot. Since mid-2025, the stock has rocketed from $15 to $127 as investors treat the company as a de facto tracking stock for SpaceX’s looming $2 trillion IPO. The catalyst? A massive $20 billion spectrum deal that swapped EchoStar's debt-ridden telecom business for a seat at Elon Musk’s table.
